skill.name: triage
skill.description: Guides maintainers through issue triage, label decisions, agent-ready briefs, and out-of-scope records for rejected feature requests.
skill.summary: Triage GitHub issues into clear workflow states and prepare durable briefs for follow-up work.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk matches, but review shows they are Markdown fences, inline code, examples, and issue-template text rather than executable Ruby, shell, or cryptographic code. The real risk is medium because the skill directs an assistant to query and update an issue tracker, write repository records under `.out-of-scope/`, and run local tests or commands during bug reproduction with maintainer oversight.

medium_findings.title: Issue Tracker Read and Write Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to query issues, post comments, apply workflow roles, and close issues. This is legitimate for triage, but it can affect external project state and should require authenticated user approval before mutations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly describes issue tracker queries and comments. The purpose is legitimate, but external state changes are clearly in scope.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Execution During Bug Reproduction
medium_findings.description: The bug workflow tells the assistant to run tests or commands before asking for more information. This is normal engineering work, but commands should stay within the repository and be reviewed when they may alter state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction to run tests or commands is direct evidence. No malicious command string is present, so the risk depends on user-approved execution context.

medium_findings.title: Repository Knowledge Base File Writes
medium_findings.description: For rejected enhancements, the skill directs the assistant to create or update `.out-of-scope/` Markdown records. This is expected behavior, but it modifies repository files and should be limited to maintainer-approved triage outcomes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write behavior is explicitly documented in both the main skill and reference guide. The file target is narrow and project-local, which limits severity.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline code names, examples, and issue text. I found no executable source file, shell script, crypto implementation, or prompt injection attempt in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is Markdown documentation and examples, not executable code. The files contain no script entrypoint or dynamic evaluation path.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Issue Tracker Mutations
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can lead an assistant to post comments, change roles, and close issues after maintainer confirmation. This is a controlled but real external action pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The mutation steps are named directly in the workflow. They are not malicious, but they require permission boundaries.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Test or Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill may run project tests or commands to reproduce bugs. This should be constrained to transparent, relevant commands in the checked-out project.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command execution instruction is explicit but generic. No concrete harmful command is provided.

content.value_statement: Issue backlogs lose context when labels, decisions, and follow-up notes drift apart. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured triage workflow for clear decisions and agent-ready briefs.
actual_capabilities: Classifies issues as bugs or enhancements with one workflow state.
Surfaces unlabeled, needs-triage, and needs-info issues that need maintainer attention.
Guides bug reproduction before asking reporters for more information.
Drafts durable agent briefs with behavior, interfaces, acceptance criteria, and scope boundaries.
Creates triage notes for reporters with established facts and specific questions.
Records rejected enhancement decisions in `.out-of-scope/` knowledge files.
limitations: Requires access to the project issue tracker and the project repository.
Depends on a provided mapping between canonical roles and actual issue labels.
Does not decide unusual state transitions without maintainer confirmation.
Does not automatically reopen old issues when an out-of-scope decision changes.

use_cases.title: Review Incoming Issues
use_cases.description: A maintainer can ask for issues needing attention and receive sorted triage buckets with concise summaries.

use_cases.title: Prepare Agent Work
use_cases.description: An engineering lead can move clear bugs or enhancements into ready-for-agent with a durable implementation brief.

use_cases.title: Track Rejected Features
use_cases.description: A product owner can preserve out-of-scope decisions so repeated feature requests use prior reasoning.

prompt_templates.title: Show Issues Needing Attention
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the triage workflow to show issues that need my attention. Group unlabeled, needs-triage, and needs-info issues separately.

prompt_templates.title: Triage One Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the triage workflow on issue #42. Read the full issue, summarize context, and recommend a category and state before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an Agent Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Move issue #42 toward ready-for-agent. Draft an agent brief with current behavior, desired behavior, key interfaces, acceptance criteria, and out-of-scope items.

prompt_templates.title: Record an Out-of-Scope Decision
prompt_templates.prompt: Triage issue #42 as an enhancement that may be out of scope. Check existing `.out-of-scope/` records, propose a decision, and wait before writing or closing anything.

output_examples.input: Show me anything that needs my attention.
output: Unlabeled: three issues, oldest first, each with a one-line summary.
Needs triage: two issues that need category and state decisions.
Needs info with reporter activity: one issue ready for re-evaluation.

output_examples.output: The skill confirms the intended label changes, asks whether to write an agent brief if needed, and prepares a brief with testable acceptance criteria.

output_examples.input: Close this rejected feature request as wontfix.
output_examples.output: The skill checks for a matching out-of-scope record, proposes an update or new record, drafts a comment, and waits for maintainer approval.

best_practices: Provide the mapping between canonical roles and real issue tracker labels before triage starts.
Require maintainer confirmation before comments, label changes, file writes, or issue closure.
Keep agent briefs behavioral and durable instead of tying them to current file paths or line numbers.
anti_patterns: Do not apply multiple category roles or conflicting state roles to one issue.
Do not ask reporters vague questions when specific missing facts can be named.
Do not reject enhancements without recording durable reasoning when the workflow requires it.

faq.question: What issue states does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses needs-triage, needs-info, ready-for-agent, ready-for-human, and wontfix as canonical states.

faq.question: Can it handle bugs and feature requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It classifies each issue as either bug or enhancement, then applies one workflow state.

faq.question: Does it post comments automatically?
faq.answer: The workflow includes posting comments, but marketplace users should require confirmation before external changes.

faq.question: What is an agent brief?
faq.answer: An agent brief is a structured issue comment that defines behavior, interfaces, acceptance criteria, and scope boundaries.

faq.question: What is the out-of-scope knowledge base?
faq.answer: It is a repository directory that records rejected enhancement concepts and links prior requests.

faq.question: Does it include executable code?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed files are Markdown instructions and examples, but the workflow may ask the assistant to run project tests.
